title: rhus oxyacantha var. albida
intro: Rhus oxyacantha var. albida, also known as White Frangula or Frangula alba, is a plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white, fragrant flowers and dark green, palmate leaves. The bark and roots of Rhus oxyacantha var. albida contain anthraquinones, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to gently purge the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Rhus oxyacantha var. albida, such as tinctures and decoctions, are often used to treat digestive issues, promote healthy bowel function, and support overall gut health.
